input.prompt:
I'm building a chat messaging screen in .NET MAUI targeting .NET 10. When the
keyboard opens, it covers my text input at the bottom of the screen. In .NET 9
I used WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize but that doesn't seem to work anymore.
How do I make the input bar move up when the keyboard appears?

input.initialPrompt:
I'm building a chat messaging screen in .NET MAUI targeting .NET 10. When the
keyboard opens, it covers my text input at the bottom of the screen. In .NET 9
I used WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize but that doesn't seem to work anymore.
How do I make the input bar move up when the keyboard appears?

data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about a .NET MAUI issue where the keyboard covers the bottom text input on Android, and the old `WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize` approach no longer works in .NET 10. This is a known change — Android deprecated `adjustResize` in API 30+, and .NET MAUI has been evolving its approach.

Let me provide a concise answer based on my knowledge:

In .NET 10 (and recent MAUI versions), `WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize` was removed/deprecated. The recommended approach is to use the `KeyboardAutoManagerScroll` behavior or handle edge-to-edge insets.


The main solutions involve using `KeyboardAutoManagerScroll`, which MAUI provides to automatically scroll content when the keyboard appears, or managing `WindowInsets` for proper edge-to-edge layout handling. The `KeyboardAutoManagerScroll` behavior should be enabled by default in recent MAUI versions and handles the keyboard overlap automatically. So the practical solutions are setting `adjustResize` in the manifest for standard layouts, relying on the built-in keyboard manager that handles scrolling automatically, or for edge-to-edge apps, manually adjusting padding through the window insets listener.
